items.headline: City addresses affordable housing protections after subsidy contracts end
items.summary: The council considered new affordability restrictions to protect existing affordable housing when project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This addresses a critical gap that could put long-term affordable housing at risk in San José.

items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: The City Council held a study session to examine planned capital improvements for the next fiscal year, which typically includes infrastructure, facility upgrades, and major projects. This is worth tracking if you want to know what physical improvements Mountain View is planning to fund.

items.headline: City introduces new tenant protection rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale City Council is considering changes to residential tenant protections under Chapter 19.71 of the municipal code. The ordinance amendment could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in the city.

items.headline: City releases Children and Youth Services Master Plan status
items.summary: San José City Council reviewed progress on a comprehensive master plan for children and youth services, including updates on youth empowerment programs and summer safety initiatives. This plan shapes how the city invests in youth programs and services over the coming years.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan moves forward
items.summary: The city presented a status report on its Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, outlining improvements to library buildings and services. This plan will determine which library locations get renovated or upgraded and how library services evolve.

items.headline: Downtown historic building seeks bonus density for seismic work
items.summary: The 405 Kipling Street property, a Category 4 historic resource, is requesting elevation to Category 2 status and 5,000 square feet of bonus floor area in exchange for historic and seismic rehabilitation improvements. This could allow the downtown building to add density while preserving its historic character.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: City Council is reviewing a proposal to rezone and redevelop the 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road properties with an eight-story building containing 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. The project would transform a Service Commercial area into a new mixed-use neighborhood with significant housing.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing for 333 Franklin Street postponed
items.summary: An administrative zoning hearing scheduled for Wednesday, April 8 has been cancelled and rescheduled for April 22, 2026. The project at 333 Franklin Street will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is confirmed, affecting a development proposal in central Mountain View.
